## 4. Make

Make (formerly Integromat) is the power user’s alternative to Zapier. It offers a more visual and granular approach to building automations, which can be a massive advantage when orchestrating complex, multi-step AI marketing workflows. While Zapier excels at simple, linear connections, Make shines when you need to handle intricate data transformations, branching logic, and error handling. [...] ### My Take

Pros:

 Powerful Automations: The visual workflow builder is best-in-class for its price point, allowing for complex and granular journey mapping.
 Balanced Platform: It strikes an excellent balance between marketing automation and a functional, light-sales CRM, making it a great all-in-one for many small businesses.

Cons:

 Tiered AI Access: Core AI features like Predictive Sending are locked behind more expensive plans, which might be a hurdle for new businesses.
 Add-on Costs: The total cost of ownership can increase significantly when you add necessary extras like SMS credits or their transactional email service, Postmark.

Website: 

## 7. Braze [...] Website: 

## 2. Gumloop

Gumloop is one of the more powerful platforms for building AI-native workflows, positioning itself as a direct competitor to Zapier but with a deeper focus on AI agents from the ground up. This is where you go when you need to construct sophisticated, multi-step AI automations that involve multiple models and complex logic, without requiring a team of developers.

results.title: 9 best marketing automation software tools in 2026 - Zapier
results.content:
Recently, Ortto has caught up to other AI-powered marketing platforms with tools like AI segmentation, customer evaluation, lead enrichment, and a model builder you can train on your website. You can also use AI to see what your subject line's open rate likelihood is based on predictive Ortto analytics. If it's not so promising, it'll suggest alternatives Ortto's proprietary AI has crafted to be even more effective.

You can then use the app's Reports tab to analyze customer engagement, revenue attribution, and the best time to send a message. You can also create custom dashboards using drag-and-drop (my favorite) that reflect progress toward shared goals. [...] ## Best marketing automation software for building safely with AI

### Zapier (Web)

Zapier, our pick for the best marketing automation software for AI orchestration

Zapier pros:

 SOC 2 Type II, SSO, SCIM, and centralized access controls
 Connects to 9,000+ apps
 Purpose-built for automation

Zapier cons:

 No native email marketing, SMS, advertising, or social media features

Zapier is a little different from the other apps on this list. Instead of serving as an email, SMS, social media, or ads server, it connects to all the other apps you use for those channels—and automates every aspect of those workflows. In short: it helps you get the most from your existing marketing tech stack.

| Landing Pages | Yes | Basic | Yes | Yes | Basic | No | No | [...] Where it falls short:

 Automation capabilities are basic compared to ActiveCampaign
 Pricing scales aggressively once you exceed free tier limits
 AI content generation is limited to email optimization (not full drafting)
 CRM features are minimal -- not a replacement for a real CRM
 Customer support on free plan is limited to email only (first 30 days)

Mailchimp is the best starting point for solo founders and tiny teams that need to send professional emails without a learning curve. Once your automation needs grow beyond basic sequences, you will likely outgrow it.

To get even more out of Canva, use Zapier's Canva integration to automate your design workflows. For example, you can create new Canva designs automatically from data in a spreadsheet, or save finished designs straight to Google Drive—and even kick off design workflows straight from your favorite AI tools. Learn more about how to automate Canva.

Canva pricing: Free plan includes 1.6M+ templates and other assets, 5GB of cloud storage, and some AI features; paid plans start at $12/month (billed annually), which include more storage and advanced AI features.

Runway pros: [...] ## Other AI marketing tools worth exploring

From lead gen to conversion, research to editing, pricing to analytics, AI has seeped into almost every area of marketing—and it only gets better from here. If you don't jump on the bandwagon, you'll fall behind, so it's important to find opportunities to use AI in your work as a marketer, regardless of the task.

Here are a few more apps to add to your AI marketing toolkit:

AI email assistants like Shortwave make quick work of surfacing emails using AI-powered email search.

AI productivity apps give you time back so you can concentrate on things that impact your work. This includes everything from project management apps to transcription software. [...] | Similarweb | AI visibility | Side-by-side SEO and GEO tracking |
| Clearscope | Content optimization | Content optimization tool for improving content quality and SEO rankings |
| Semrush | SEO and competitor analysis | AI Toolkit monitors brand mentions across AI on top of full SEO coverage |
| FeedHive | Social media management | Social media management tool with AI-powered content recycling and conditional posting |
| ActiveCampaign | Email marketing automation | Marketing automation platform for drip campaigns and behavior-based journeys |
| HubSpot CRM | AI CRM | CRM for aligning marketing and sales with built-in AI tools |
| Cursor | Vibe coding | Coding assistant for building marketing experiments, landing pages, and internal tools |
